remember: Students will be able to recall and list the types of exceptions in Java.
Students will be able to define and explain the concept of try-catch block.
Students will be able to identify the different methods of exception handling in Java.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of exception and its importance in programming.
Students will be able to explain how exceptions are thrown and caught in Java.
Students will be able to differentiate between checked and unchecked exceptions.
apply: Students will be able to write a Java program that uses try-catch block to handle exceptions.
Students will be able to use multiple catch blocks to handle different types of exceptions.
Students will be able to use the finally block to execute code regardless of the exception.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the code to identify potential exception handling issues.
Students will be able to suggest improvements to exception handling in a given Java program.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different exception handling techniques.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of exceptions and exception handling in Java.
Differentiate between checked and unchecked exceptions in Java.
Use try-catch-finally blocks to handle exceptions in Java.
board_exam_objectives: Identify and handle ArithmeticException, NullPointerException, ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException, and ClassCastException in Java.
Use try-catch block to handle exceptions in Java programming.
Write a Java program that uses exception handling to prevent program termination.
